decision_statement: How should ProductOS create differentiated strategic value in discovery rather than only execution efficiency?

options.title: Double down on trust and traceability
options.option_thesis: Win by becoming the most trusted operating layer for AI-assisted PM decisions.
options.upside_case: Enterprise buyers adopt because explainability and governance are first-class.
options.failure_mode: The product becomes credible but insufficiently differentiated in strategic insight.
options.portfolio_interaction: Strengthens governance and reliability packs without changing product shape drastically.
options.org_capability_requirement: Strong schema, validation, and auditability discipline.

options.title: Lead with strategic sensing
options.option_thesis: Win by helping PMs detect opportunity shifts and risks earlier than competitors.
options.upside_case: ProductOS becomes the system PMs use to generate non-obvious strategic insight.
options.failure_mode: Signals stay noisy and users do not trust the recommendations enough to act.
options.portfolio_interaction: Creates pull for radar, research, and portfolio intelligence capabilities.
options.org_capability_requirement: Strong synthesis, signal evaluation, and ambiguity management.

options.title: Differentiate through prototype-led strategy
options.option_thesis: Win by making concept validation and decision simulation dramatically more concrete than rivals.
options.upside_case: Ambiguous strategy discussions move faster because the system makes tradeoffs tangible.
options.failure_mode: The product is seen as a better prototyping system rather than a full PM operating system.
options.portfolio_interaction: Builds on existing prototype-first discovery and communication strengths.
options.org_capability_requirement: Fast prototype generation and tight feedback synthesis loops.
